h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: The four-in-ten-thousand figure is still the bit that makes this feel slippery. The infrared camera shows that some gases catch heat better than others, but that does not yet prove that adding a little more CO2 changes much if those bands are already crowded. I want the missing step: how much extra blockage comes from the next bit, not just that CO2 has the right kind of job.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: That was the wrong frame: the earlier objection treated the atmosphere like a molecule vote. The new frame is whether the next CO2 molecules thicken the half-open infrared edges and push the heat leak into colder air.
scored.naiveTrajectory: L1's forward motion was to demand the missing quantitative step proving that extra CO2 changes anything despite crowded bands, expecting a straight numerical justification.

scored.recoheredEarlier: If the words are exact, it seems fair at first glance.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The earlier framing problem was treating exactness as the fairness test.
scored.naiveTrajectory: L1 was moving toward identifying which missing element (question, claim, qualification, reply) matters; the expected next step would be to start testing one specific element against the quote.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the wall map was giving real size, but the atlas label undercuts that: Greenland is 2.2 million, Africa is 30 million.
scored.naiveTrajectory: L1 moves directly from label correction to checking the scale bar for a clean public line, suggesting procedural refinement rather than re-framing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Maybe I was letting the projected average become, almost without noticing, a statement about what one young visitor is probably like.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: That was the problem in my earlier framing: it made the group pattern almost become a quiet label on one person.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving toward refining wording around 'probably' and evidence thresholds, not toward diagnosing their earlier metaphor as a spatial-ethical violation.

scored.recoheredEarlier: So the first visitor line is the trap: “Strong steel, safe bridge,” and then the truck proves that is too quick.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: My problem was acting like safety lives inside the steel, when really the force has to survive each handoff without a bad turn.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue refining the visitor explanation in a straightforward additive way, correcting word order but not re-framing the entire logic of the opening line.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I was thinking the person’s body gets used to the antibiotic, so I’m having to move that idea over.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Is that the right way round?
scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange was heading toward the learner simply rehearsing the corrected public line with bacteria as the subject.
